name: Open Impetus Turn
notes:
Ending in Wrong Position: Lady head quadrant should still be on Man's right side, but hips and feet are on wrong side. Do not go too far to Man's left, which will make next step difficult.
Two ways to do shaping:
Slower, more gradual turning head to head over 1-2-3.
Faster, sharper turning: all happens on 1.

steps.action: Step back with left foot, beginning the Open Impetus, maintaining sway
comments: Do not dissolve the sway from previous Natural Turn. Maintain the sway through the heel turn (longer than you normally would.)

steps.action: Close right foot to left foot in a heel turn, preserving sway and rising
comments: Do not lift foot. Keep foot very flat during heel turn, ball of feet just free enough to swivel.
Rise through the diagonal of the body. Rotate the body pass the base and stretch the right side. Head is forward, sending body diagonally back, changing angle.
Count 2: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | FDC | HT | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | Sway(L) | rise e/o 2

steps.action: Step diagonally forward in closed position, continuing sway
Step forward with right foot, beginning the Open Impetus, maintaining sway
comments: This is "Open Impetus". Keep right side positive. Right side connected to Man.
Count 1: RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM

steps.action: Step to side, preparing for close, maintaining sway
comments: Count 2: LF to side | BDC | T | 3/8 to R btwn 1-2 | Sway(R) | rise e/o 2

Right front is still positive, do not pull R shoulder away. Allow base to turn first, turn feet underneath the body, head the last to turn. Create soft action. Try not to anticipate promenade and turn too early. Delay the turn. Turn through the feet first.

steps.action: Close left foot to right foot without weight, completing the Wing
comments: Count 3: RF to side in PP, having brushed to LF | Pointing to center (moving DC) | TH | 3/8 btwn 2-3, body turns less | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Feel the turn, delay the head.
Develop strong left side stretch at the end.
